model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Even allowing for some rotation uncertainty, UConn’s baseline quality as a 2-seed and its typical two-way profile (size, rim protection, and physical defense translating well in a one-off) should overwhelm a Furman team that rates around the ~190 range in both KenPom and Torvik. Furman’s upset path is mostly three-point variance and forcing UConn into a sloppy, high-turnover game, but its own defensive efficiency numbers suggest it may struggle to string together enough stops if UConn plays to form. With recent reporting indicating at least one of UConn’s key injury situations is trending more positive, I’m sticking with the heavy favorite.

reasoningStep.title: East R64 (2/15): UConn vs Furman
reasoningStep.summary: UConn advances based on a large underlying team-quality gap; Furman needs extreme shooting/turnover variance to threaten.
evidence: Furman ratings: KenPom #191 (AdjEM -1.99; AdjO 107.5 #200; AdjD 109.5), Torvik #195 (AdjO 106.84; AdjD 108.98).
Injury context: local reporting (CT Insider) noted UConn had injury updates on Jaylin Stewart and Silas Demary Jr. entering the Furman matchup, with indications at least one situation was improving.
Matchup logic: 15-seed upset path is primarily 3-point/turnover variance; Furman’s below-average national efficiency on both ends makes sustaining an upset difficult if UConn plays to form.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://www.ctinsider.com/sports/uconn-mens-basketball/article/injury-news-dan-hurley-silas-demary-jaylin-stewart-22080303.php and extracted 3000 characters of text.

result.text: UConn's Dan Hurley gives injury updates before NCAA Tournament Ct Insider Logo Hearst Newspapers Logo Skip to main content Subscribe Home Contact Us Advertise With Us Newsletters FAQ Privacy Notice Terms of Use E-Edition Our Brands Cheshire Herald Connecticut Magazine County Times Fairfield Citizen Journal Inquirer New Milford Spectrum Record-Journal Shoreline Times West Hartford News Westport News Waterbury Republican-American News Education Politics Election Health Latino News Sports GametimeCT UConn UConn Women's Basketball UConn Men's Basketball College Connecticut Sun WNBA NFL NBA MLB Hockey Business Entertainment Horoscopes Advice Puzzles Comics Real Estate Living Food Weather Opinion We Asked, You Answered Obits Best Of Education Home Services Legal Services Senior Living Entertainment Food & Drink Health Advertising Features Events Jobs Classifieds Ct Insider Logo Subscribe Trending: Speed cameras on highways? | Gusty winds for St Patrick's Day | CT power outages | CT pension spending | Horoscopes Sports // UConn Men's Basketball Dan Hurley gives injury updates on UConn's Jaylin Stewart, Silas Demary Jr. ahead of NCAA Tournament By Declan Walsh , Staff Writer March 16, 2026 STORRS, CONNECTICUT - NOVEMBER 19: Silas Demary Jr. #2 and Jaylin Stewart #3 of the Connecticut Huskies dive for the loose ball against Brayden Burries #5 of the Arizona Wildcats during the first half of an NCAA men's basketball game. Joe Buglewicz/Getty Images The UConn men's basketball team prepares for the upcoming NCAA Tournament potentially low on manpower with the injury statuses of two key contributors still in question ahead of Friday's opener against Furman. Jaylin Stewart has not played since Feb. 21, as the junior wing continues to recover from an unspecified knee injury, while Silas Demary Jr. was forced to exit Saturday's Big East Tournament final with a left ankle sprain . Dan Hurley provided updates on both players while speaking with the media Sunday evening. Advertisement Article continues below this ad "Health-wise, it will be close with Jaylin," Hurley said. The UConn coach provided a more promising update on his star point guard: "I think Silas is in pretty good shape." Jaylin Stewart has faced a mercurial junior season, starting UConn's first 10 games this season as freshman wing Braylon Mullins battled a pre-season injury — the Seattle native delivered double-digit scoring performances against Arizona and Texas. Stewart returned to an off-the-bench role once Mullins hit his stride around the New Year, but he stepped up with a crucial pair of 3-pointers against Villanova in his final game before the ongoing injury saga. More For You UConn stars Sarah Strong, Azzi Fudd named to USBWA All-America team The team is selected by members of the United States Basketball Writers Association. Rebecca Lobo’s March Madness pick? An elite takeout order UConn icon Rebecca Lobo picks her go-to

result.text: UConn Basketball Faces Injury Concerns Ahead of NCAA Tournament - Philadelphia Today Skip to content National Today Menu National Today Today Holidays Animals Arts & Entertainment Cause Cultural Federal Food & Beverage Health Relationships Religious Special Interest Birthdays January February March April May June July August September October November December Reminders Cities Atlanta Austin Baltimore Berwyn Beverly Hills Birmingham Boston Brooklyn Buffalo Charlotte Chicago Cincinnati Cleveland Columbus Dallas Denver Detroit Fort Worth Houston Indianapolis Knoxville Las Vegas Los Angeles Louisville Madison Memphis Miami Milwaukee Minneapolis Nashville New Orleans New York Omaha Orlando Philadelphia Phoenix Pittsburgh Portland Raleigh Richmond Rutherford Sacramento Salt Lake City San Antonio San Diego San Francisco San Jose Seattle Tampa Tucson Washington Log in Sign up Search Search Cancel Search National Today Submit Philadelphia Today By the People, for the People News Sports Basketball Baseball Body Building Boxing Cycling Equestrian eSports Extreme Sports Football Golf Gymnastics Hockey Ice Skating Mixed Martial Arts Motorsports Olympics Other Sports Rodeo Running Skiing Soccer Softball Surfing Swimming Tennis Track & Field Volleyball Wrestling More UConn Basketball Faces Injury Concerns Ahead of NCAA Tournament Head coach Dan Hurley provides updates on key players' status for the first round Mar. 16, 2026 at 3:52am Got story updates? Submit your updates here. › UConn basketball is heading into the 2026 NCAA Tournament with some injury concerns. Head coach Dan Hurley shared updates on the status of two key players - forward Jaylin Stewart and guard Silas Demary Jr. Stewart's status for the first round is uncertain, while Demary is reported to be 'in pretty good shape'. The Huskies are the No. 2 seed and will face the No. 15 Furman Paladins on Friday in Philadelphia. Why it matters UConn is looking to reclaim the national title after missing out on a three-peat in 2025. The health of Stewart and Demary could be crucial to the Huskies' chances of making a deep tournament run. Demary in particular is a key contributor, leading the team in assists and steals. The details Forward Jaylin Stewart has not played since a Feb. 21 game against Villanova and missed the entire Big East tournament. Coach Hurley said it 'will be close' on whether Stewart can play in the first round. Meanwhile, junior guard Silas Demary Jr. is reported to be 'in pretty good shape'. Demary is second on the team in usage and leads the Huskies in assists and steals. Stewart has not played since a Feb. 21 game against Villanova. UConn will face Furman in the first round on Friday, March 21, 2026. The players Dan Hurley The head coach of the UConn Huskies men's basketball team. Jaylin Stewart A junior forward for the UConn Huskies who has been sidelined with an injury. Silas Demary Jr. A junior guard for the UConn Huskies who is reported to be in 'pretty good shape' heading into

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
